text: Moving from the empty glass rooms of ordered modernity into this expressive space, I feel a palpable shift in atmosphere. The R+D wall, busy with words and images, invites reflection more than the blank meeting rooms do. Here, surrounded by inquiry in text and screen, I sense a call to question and interpret. The hum of electronics and the deliberate ambiguity of the displays evoke curiosity and a faint unease, as if every thought here is being recorded, dissected, analyzed by some unseen presence. It is stimulating but uncanny, both inspiring and slightly disquieting, as if I am at once participant and object of study.
